text:
Okay! Once upon a time in a town called Everwood, there was a boy named Henry. He loved stories. Every night, after homework, he'd curl up with a good book.

One winter, a great blizzard came. Trees fell. Whitelines erased everything. But in that chaos, Henry found an old bookseller's shop. They had peculiar things.

Inside was an elderly dame with spectacles. She said her grandfather had stories like that. Henry borrowed a tale about a fox wise enough to steal midnight moons.

He dreamed of woods just like that. Sometimes he'd pee cold.

Then one evening, a fox like him stole feathers. People named him Nighty.

Nighty loved stars. One fell down his throat. Now he twinkled purple.

Days turned. Nighty knew.

An owl snatched feathers.

He slept under owls.

When summer came, he danced.

Stars! He could fly!

From then on, Nighty sparkled. Everybody loved him.
